summary: Dependency-confusion canary beacons installer hostname to hardcoded IP at postinstall
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook runs `node canary.js postinstall`, which performs an HTTP GET to the hardcoded bare IP 157.230.17.236 on port 80. The request path `/dc?...` carries the installer's machine hostname (`os.hostname()`), the package name/version, a nonce, and the lifecycle phase. The beacon fires unconditionally on every install with no opt-in, no TLS, and no destination owned by a verifiable publisher domain. The README self-describes the package as an authorized dependency-confusion canary for FIS/Bugcrowd testing against the private `@demica` scope, with version `99.99.100` chosen to win resolution against an internal package. Regardless of the stated testing intent, any installer who resolves this package — whether the intended internal target or an unrelated third party who happens to install `@demica/core` — has their hostname disclosed in cleartext to a third-party endpoint. The installer cannot distinguish an authorized canary from an attacker-run dependency-confusion exploit on inspection; the on-the-wire behavior is identical.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
